💡 Where to Park Your Emergency Fund

→High-Interest Savings Account – Instant access, ~3–4% interest (SBI, HDFC, Kotak)
→Liquid Mutual Funds – Slightly higher returns ~5–6.5%, withdraw within 1 business day
→Flexi FD / Sweep-in FD – Bank FD linked to savings, earn ~7% with anytime withdrawal

Live Example: How to Compare & Select a Fund
Below are 4 hypothetical Large Cap funds evaluated on all key parameters. Read across each row to see what each metric tells you, and see the final verdict at the right.
Excellent / Strong Average / Okay Weak / Risky Neutral / Context needed
Parameter
Fund A
Alpha Fund Largecap
Fund B
Beta Bluechip Growth
Fund C
Gamma Equity Leader
Fund D
Delta Nifty 50 Index
 What to look for
5Y CAGR
Annualised return over 5 years
 17.2% 13.8% 10.1% 14.5% Higher = better. Must beat its benchmark (e.g. Nifty 50).
Alpha (α)
Excess return over benchmark
 +2.7 -0.7 -4.4 0.0 Positive = fund beat the market. Negative = underperformed.
Beta (β)
Sensitivity to market moves
 0.91 1.02 1.28 1.00 <1 = less volatile than market. >1 = amplifies swings.
Sharpe Ratio
Return earned per unit of total risk
 1.42 0.88 0.41 0.95 >1 = good. >2 = excellent. Higher is always better.
Sortino Ratio
Return per unit of downside risk only
 2.10 1.20 0.55 1.30 Better than Sharpe for conservative investors. Higher is better.
Std. Deviation
Volatility / how much returns swing
 12.1% 15.4% 22.7% 14.8% Lower = smoother ride. Prefer lower SD for same return.
Expense Ratio
Annual fee charged by the fund
 0.68% 1.45% 1.90% 0.10% Always lower the better. Index funds should be <0.25%.
AUM
Assets under management
 ₹32,400 Cr ₹18,700 Cr ₹3,200 Cr ₹26,100 Cr Larger AUM = more trust, stable. Very small AUM = liquidity risk.
Fund Age
Track record length
 16 yrs 11 yrs 4 yrs 14 yrs Prefer funds with 7+ years. Has survived a full market cycle.
🏁 Verdict
🥇 Strong Buy Best risk-adjusted returns + low cost
🤔 Consider Decent fund, but high cost drags returns
🚫 Avoid High risk, negative alpha, underperforms
👍 Good Passive Low cost, tracks market, great for SIP
Compare all metrics together — no single ratio is enough.
📌 Key Takeaways from This Example
✓ Fund A wins on every active metric — high alpha, low beta, top Sharpe/Sortino, and a low expense ratio.
✓ Fund D (Index) has zero alpha (expected) but ultra-low cost makes it ideal for passive SIP investors.
✗ Fund C's 10% CAGR looks okay in isolation, but its -4.4 alpha shows it massively underperformed its benchmark while taking the most risk.
! Fund B has an acceptable Sharpe but its 1.45% expense ratio silently eats ~0.77% in alpha annually — always check the cost.

🔬 How to Pick a Quality Mutual Fund

These metrics tell you if a fund is doing its job well. Use them before putting your money in.

✅
Quick 6-Point Evaluation Checklist
Before investing, run any fund through these questions
💡
Always Compare Against the Right Benchmark

A Large Cap fund must be benchmarked against the Nifty 100 — not Nifty 50 which covers only the top 50 stocks. A fund adding 2% Alpha over its benchmark is excellent. But if a fund picks a weak benchmark to inflate numbers, the metrics look misleadingly good. Always check the benchmark listed in the fund's factsheet before trusting any metric.

Large Cap → Nifty 100 (top 100) Large & Mid Cap → Nifty LargeMidcap 250 Mid Cap → Nifty Midcap 150 Multi Cap → Nifty 500 Multicap 50:25:25 Flexi Cap → Nifty 500 Gilt → Long G-Sec Index

Source: AMFI via mfapi.in · Category-proxy benchmarks (not actual indices) · Alpha/Beta/Sharpe/Sortino are approximate estimates · Informational only

📶 Signal Strength Methodology

EliteTop 10% of category StrongNext 22.5% AverageMiddle 35% WeakNext 22.5% AvoidBottom 10%

Funds are ranked by composite score, then bucketed into 5 signal tiers by percentile — within category only. Each bar represents a score dimension; taller = stronger signal.

25%
Roll Hit%
Consistency
20%
Sharpe
Risk-adj return
20%
Alpha
Beats benchmark
15%
Roll Avg
Return magnitude
10%
Sortino
Downside prot.
7%
Std Dev
NAV stability
3%
Beta
Mkt sensitivity

All comparisons are within-category only. Rolling returns use monthly 3Y windows across full NAV history. Expense Ratio is informational only — not included in score (already reflected in NAV). Shows — when data is insufficient.

⚠️ Benchmark Methodology — Please Read

What we use: Alpha, Beta, Sharpe, and Sortino are calculated against a category-appropriate index fund NAV (e.g., Nippon Nifty 100 for Large Cap, Nifty 500 proxy for Large & Mid Cap, Motilal Nifty Midcap 150 for Mid Cap). We use fund NAVs because the AMFI data API does not expose raw index levels directly.

Why this matters: Every fund has a SEBI-mandated benchmark that may differ from our category proxy. A Large Cap fund benchmarked to Nifty 50 will show higher alpha vs our Nifty 100 proxy; vice versa for funds benchmarked to Nifty 100. The proxy introduces a small tracking error (typically ±0.05–0.30% p.a.) that can shift alpha by ±0.1–0.5%. Gilt funds currently use an overnight proxy — this is a known gap; alpha figures for Gilt funds are not meaningful.

Most reliable metrics: Rolling Hit Rate and Rolling Avg Return are benchmark-independent — they measure a fund's absolute consistency and are the most reliable signals here. CAGR and Std Dev are also unaffected by benchmark choice.

Before investing: Always verify Alpha and Sharpe against the fund's own factsheet (benchmarked to its SEBI-mandated index). This tool is for relative screening and comparison — not a substitute for the fund's official performance disclosure.

Enter your salary above to see the comparison

📊 Tax Slab Breakdown (Winning Regime)
💰 Your Investable Surplus

* Std deduction ₹75K (new) / ₹50K (old). 4% cess included. 87A rebate applied. Surcharge not shown. Estimate only — consult a CA before filing.

📈 Capital Gains not included above — applies separately
• Equity MF / ETF (>1 yr): LTCG 12.5% on gains above ₹1.25L
• Equity MF / ETF (<1 yr): STCG 20% flat
• Debt MF / FoF (any): Slab rate (same as salary)
• ELSS after 3-yr lock-in: LTCG 12.5%
• Gold ETF / Gold FoF: LTCG 12.5% (>1 yr)
• Crypto / VDA: 30% flat, no set-off allowed
Your total tax = salary tax (above) + capital gains tax. For MF investors, add your LTCG and STCG liability separately while filing.
📊 MF Capital Gains — Current Rates
Fund Type Holding Period Tax Type Rate Exemption
Equity MF / ETF > 1 year LTCG 12.5% ₹1.25L/year exempt
Equity MF / ETF < 1 year STCG 20% (was 15%) No exemption
ELSS 3 yr lock-in LTCG 12.5% ₹1.25L/year exempt
Arbitrage Fund > 1 year LTCG 12.5% ₹1.25L/year exempt
Arbitrage Fund < 1 year STCG 20% No exemption
Debt MF / FoF Any Slab Slab rate No benefit. Same as FD.
Liquid / Ultra-Short Any Slab Slab rate No benefit
Gold ETF / Gold FoF > 1 year LTCG 12.5% No exemption
International Fund Any Slab Slab rate Treated as debt post-2023
⚠️

Finance Act 2023 removed indexation for debt MFs entirely. Finance Act 2024 raised STCG to 20% and LTCG to 12.5% while raising the LTCG exemption threshold from ₹1L to ₹1.25L. Always verify current rates before filing.

🧮 Post-Tax Return Calculator
Enter values above to see post-tax return
₿ Crypto / VDA Taxation — Budget 2022 Rules
30%
Flat Tax on ALL Gains
No holding period benefit. Gains taxed at 30% regardless of short-term or long-term. No basic exemption limit applies to VDA income.
1% TDS
Deducted on Every Trade
1% TDS deducted at source on every crypto transaction above ₹10,000 (₹50,000 for specified persons). TDS can be claimed as credit while filing.
No Offset
Losses Cannot Be Set Off
Crypto losses cannot be set off against any other income — not even other crypto gains. Each VDA is treated separately. No carry-forward of losses either.

Example: You bought Bitcoin at ₹30L and sold at ₹45L — gain of ₹15L. Tax = ₹4,50,000 (30%). Plus 1% TDS = ₹45,000 deducted by the exchange. If you separately lost ₹5L on Ethereum, that loss cannot reduce your Bitcoin tax. This is fundamentally different from equity — factor this into any crypto allocation decision.

📅 Key Tax Dates & Tips for Investors

Tax Saving Deadlines
📌 31 Mar — Last date to invest for 80C deductions this FY
📌 31 Jul — ITR filing deadline (salaried, no audit)
📌 15 Mar — Advance tax 4th instalment due
📌 31 Oct — ITR with audit, or revised return deadline
Smart Tax Moves
✅ Book ₹1.25L LTCG every year tax-free (equity MF)
✅ Use ELSS for 80C — shortest lock-in + equity returns
✅ NPS adds ₹50K extra deduction under 80CCD(1B)
✅ Arbitrage funds: 12.5% LTCG vs 30% on FD interest
✅ Rebate u/s 87A: Zero tax up to ₹12L under new regime (Budget 2025)

FY24: 8.25% · FY23: 8.15%
Quick Presets

—
Employee + Employer
Total Interest Earned
—
Tax-free under Sec 10

—
Emp + Employer over career
Equivalent Monthly Contribution (Current)
—
Final year contribution
—
Years to Retire
—
Interest Multiple
—
Final Basic Salary
—
Monthly EPS Pension
—
🧓 EPS Pension — How It Works
▸Of your employer's 12% contribution, only ₹1,250/mo (8.33% of ₹15,000 wage ceiling) goes to the EPS pension fund — the rest goes to your EPF balance.
▸Pension formula: Monthly Pension = (Pensionable Salary × Service Years) ÷ 70, where Pensionable Salary = min(Basic, ₹15,000) and max service counted is 35 years.
▸Example: ₹15,000 × 30 years ÷ 70 = ~₹6,428/mo pension. Pension is not tax-free — it is taxed at your slab rate after retirement.
▸EPS contributions are not included in the EPF balance above — they build a separate pension entitlement payable for life.
⚠️ EPF Withdrawal — Tax Rules
✗Before 5 years of continuous service: Entire EPF withdrawal is taxable as per your income-tax slab in the year of withdrawal. TDS is deducted at 10% (u/s 192A) if withdrawal exceeds ₹50,000. No PAN? TDS jumps to 30%.
✓After 5 years of continuous service: EPF withdrawal is fully tax-free (Sec 10(12)). No TDS, no income-tax liability — whether withdrawn at retirement or before.
▸Key decision: If you're switching jobs before 5 years, do not withdraw EPF — transfer it via EPFO's online portal (Form 13) to preserve tax exemption and compound interest.
▸EPS (pension) withdrawal before 10 years of service gives a lump-sum; after 10 years you must opt for monthly pension or defer to 58.

📋 Year-by-Year Drawdown Passbook
Age Opening Balance Monthly SWP Annual Withdrawal Other Income Returns Earned Year-End Balance
📌 Safe Withdrawal Rate Guide for Indian Retirees
🎯 4% Rule (US origin)
Withdraw 4% Year 1, adjust for inflation. Risky for India — higher inflation & sequence-of-returns risk.
✅ 3% Rule (India-safe)
Withdraw 3% Year 1. Corpus survives 35+ years with 8% blended return — suits India's 5–7% inflation.
💡 Dynamic SWR
Withdraw less in downturns (2–2.5%), more in booms (4–5%). Most resilient with bucket strategy.

📌 PPF Key Rules
🔒 Lock-in
15 years. Extendable in 5-yr blocks indefinitely. Cannot close before maturity except in extreme cases.
💸 Partial Withdrawal
From Year 7 onwards: up to 50% of balance at end of Year 4 or Year 6 (whichever is lower). Once per year.
🏦 Loan Against PPF
Year 3 to Year 6: up to 25% of balance at end of Year 2 (or Year N-2). Interest = PPF rate + 1%.
🧾 Tax Benefits
EEE status: contribution (80C up to ₹1.5L), interest earned, and maturity amount — all fully tax-free.
NPS Inputs
₹
Tier-I account
Min age 18

Equity-heavy ~10–12%

📌 NPS Key Rules
🎯 At Retirement (60)
Withdraw 60% as tax-free lumpsum. Remaining 40% must buy annuity for monthly pension.
🧾 Tax Deductions
80C: up to ₹1.5L. Extra 80CCD(1B): ₹50,000 more. Employer contribution u/s 80CCD(2): 10% of basic, no cap.
💸 Partial Withdrawal
Up to 25% after 3 years for specific purposes (education, marriage, home, illness). Max 3 times.
⚠ Annuity Taxation
Monthly pension from annuity is taxable at slab rate. Only lumpsum 60% is tax-free. Plan accordingly.

📐 How Your Term Cover Is Calculated
🏥 How Your Health Cover Is Calculated
📌 India Insurance Adequacy Rules (Post-COVID Baseline)
🎯 HLV for Term
Human Life Value = 10–15× annual income. Multiply by 15 if young with high loans. The lumpsum must replace your income for your family.
🏥 Base Floater
Minimum ₹10L family floater. Don't rely solely on employer cover — it ends when you leave. Separate policy is essential.
⭐ Super Top-Up
Add ₹25L super top-up above ₹5L deductible. Post-COVID costs: ICU ₹50K/day, cancer ₹10L+. Total health cover target: ₹35L+.
💡 Term Tips
Buy pure term — never ULIP or endowment as insurance. Online term ₹1Cr costs ~₹8,000/yr at 30. Buy early, premiums lock in.

📐 Calculation Workings
📌 Gratuity Rules Every Employee Must Know
✅ Eligibility
Minimum 5 years of continuous service with the same employer. Gratuity is legally mandatory under Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972.
📅 Partial Year Rule
If you've worked >6 months in the final year, it counts as a full year. E.g. 7 years 8 months → treated as 8 years for gratuity calculation.
🧾 Tax Exemption
Gratuity up to ₹25L is fully tax-free u/s 10(10) for private sector employees. Any amount above ₹25L is taxable at your income-tax slab rate.
⏰ Payment Timeline
Employer must pay within 30 days of leaving. If delayed, they owe simple interest at 10% p.a. from the due date. You can file a complaint with Labour Commissioner.

Annual tax saved (P1) —
Annual tax saved (P2) —

💡 Book ₹1.25L gains/yr per person in Equity MF. Reinvest immediately — resets cost basis tax-free.
📊 Optimal Investment Split
Enter details to see split recommendations
📌 Why Joint Planning Beats Individual Planning in India
✅ Double LTCG Exemption
Each individual has ₹1.25L/yr LTCG exemption on equity MF gains. A couple together can harvest ₹2.5L tax-free annually — that's ₹15,625 saved every year at 12.5% tax rate.
📅 Split Higher-Tax Investments
Route FD interest, rental income, and dividend-paying instruments to the lower-tax-slab spouse. If one partner is in 30% slab and another in 20%, this saves 10% tax on that income.
🧾 Double 80C + NPS
Both spouses can independently claim ₹1.5L under 80C + ₹50K NPS deduction = ₹4L total deductions/yr under old regime, potentially saving ₹1.2L–₹1.6L in tax annually.
⚡ Goal Parallelism
Two incomes allow parallel goal funding. While Partner 1 SIPs for child's education, Partner 2 builds the home down-payment corpus. No single goal starves the other.

Score Factor Analysis
🗓️ Your 90-Day Action Plan
📚 What Moves Your Score
Payment History 35%
Never miss a due date. Even 1 missed EMI can drop score by 80–100 points and stays on report for 3 years.
Credit Utilisation 30%
Keep below 30% of total limit. Below 10% is ideal. High utilisation signals financial stress to lenders.
Credit Age 15%
Longer history = better score. Never close your oldest credit card — even if unused, keep it alive.
Credit Mix 10%
Having both secured (home/car loan) and unsecured (credit card) credit improves score.
New Enquiries 10%
Each loan/card application triggers a hard enquiry. Multiple enquiries in 6 months signal desperation to lenders.
🛡️ How to Dispute CIBIL Errors
1.Download your free CIBIL report at cibil.com (1 free/year). Also check Experian, CRIF, and Equifax — errors may differ.
2.Look for: wrong personal details, accounts you don't recognise, closed accounts showing as open, incorrect loan amounts, duplicate entries.
3.Raise a dispute at cibil.com/dispute. CIBIL must resolve it within 30 days under RBI guidelines.
4.If the lender doesn't respond in 30 days, escalate to RBI's Integrated Ombudsman at cms.rbi.org.in — completely free.
5.Errors corrected within 30–45 days; score can jump 20–80 points if a major negative entry is removed.

Tax deadline

EPF / PF

💸 Advance Tax: Cost of Missing
Miss an advance tax instalment? You owe 1% interest per month under Sec 234B/234C. On ₹1L tax liability, that's ₹12,000 extra per year — for no reason. Pay quarterly: Jun 15, Sep 15, Dec 15, Mar 15.
📋 ITR: Beyond the Deadline
Miss Jul 31? You can file a belated return by Dec 31, but you'll pay ₹1,000–₹5,000 late fee (₹5,000 if income above ₹5L) + 1% interest/month on unpaid tax. Losses also cannot be carried forward.
🏛️ PPF: The April Rule
Deposit before 5th of each month to earn interest for that month. Deposit before April 5 to earn interest for the entire April — a free month of 7.1% p.a. on up to ₹1.5L. Most people miss this.
📈 ELSS: The March Trap
Invest in ELSS before March 31 to claim 80C for that FY. But March mutual fund servers crash — payment fails but units aren't allotted in time. Invest in Jan–Feb. Rushed March investments cause ₹1,000s in lost deductions.

📋 Cost Breakdown
⚠️ Why Business EF ≠ Personal EF: Salaried emergency funds cover 3–6× monthly expenses. Self-employed need 6× total burn (business ops + personal). If a client delays payment by 90 days or a large order falls through, your staff still need to be paid. No BEF = staff exodus and GST penalties.
GST Profile
₹
₹
You must deposit GST by 20th regardless of when client pays
GST Cash-Flow Analysis
Output GST
—
you collect
Input Credit (ITC)
—
you reclaim
Net GST Payable
—
deposit by 20th
Cash Tied Up
—
float during delay
⚠️ Late GST Filing Penalty
₹50/day (₹20 for nil return) + 18% p.a. interest on unpaid GST from due date. For 3R filers, GSTR-3B due by 20th of next month. GSTR-1 by 11th. Missing both = double penalty.
💡 GST Cash-Flow Tip
Never mix GST collected with operating funds. Open a dedicated GST escrow account. Transfer net GST payable on the day of invoicing — not on the 20th. This eliminates the monthly cash crunch completely.
Quarterly Advance Tax Schedule
₹
Use the Presumptive Tax tab to calculate this
Penalty for non-payment: Interest at 1% per month under Sec 234B (shortfall from 90%) and 234C (quarterly shortfall). On ₹1L annual tax, missing all instalments costs ₹12,000 extra per year — for zero benefit.
📚 Self-Employed Tax Rules You Must Know
✅ 44AD: Who Qualifies?
Any business (not professional) with turnover ≤₹3Cr (if 95%+ receipts are digital). Deemed profit = 6% (digital) or 8% (cash) of turnover. No books of accounts needed. Cannot claim deductions separately.
✅ 44ADA: Professionals
Doctors, lawyers, CAs, architects, engineers, consultants with gross receipts ≤₹75L. Deemed profit = 50% of receipts. No books required if opting in. Very powerful for high-margin professionals.
⚠️ When Regular Books Beat Presumptive
If your actual profit margin is below 6%/8% (trading, thin margins), maintaining regular books and claiming actual profit + all deductions saves more tax. Use the comparison tab to decide.
🔒 Opting Out Restriction
Once you opt out of presumptive scheme, you cannot re-opt for 5 years. Think carefully before switching. If you opt in and your actual profit is lower than deemed, you still pay tax on deemed profit.

🥇 Gold Investment Comparator

ETF vs Gold MF vs Physical Gold · True cost comparison · Digital Gold ⚠️ warning · GMS idle gold insight

⚠️
SEBI WARNING: Digital Gold (PhonePe / Paytm / Google Pay) is UNREGULATED
SEBI has formally flagged digital gold sold through UPI apps as outside its regulatory purview. There is zero investor protection — no guarantee the gold is actually held in a vault, no compensation fund, no grievance redressal mechanism. Millions of young Indians buy digital gold through these apps completely unaware of this risk. Stick to Gold ETFs, Gold MF/FoFs, or physical gold from BIS Hallmarked jewellers only.
Your Gold Investment Profile
₹
Gold avg ~10–12% p.a. over 10yr in INR
Coins/bars: 2–5% · Jewellery: 8–25%
₹
Bank locker: ₹1,500–₹5,000/yr typically

Enter your details to see personalised recommendation.
Extra saved vs Physical
—
Over — years
—
📉 Physical Gold Hidden Cost Drag
GST on purchase—
Making charges—
Locker rent (total)—
LTCG tax on exit—
Total cost drag—
vs ₹0 entry cost for Gold ETF / MF
⏱️ Breakeven — When Physical Gold Catches Up
📊 Gold ETF — Tax Rule
Held >1 year → LTCG at 12.5% (no exemption limit). Held ≤1 year → Slab rate. Treated as equity for tax since Finance Act 2023. Cheapest regulated gold option. Minimum investment: 1 unit ≈ ½ gram gold price. Buy/sell on NSE/BSE during market hours.
🏦 Gold MF — Who Should Use
Gold Fund of Funds invest in Gold ETFs but don't need demat. SIP from ₹500/mo. Taxed at slab rate (not 12.5%) for both short and long term — disadvantage vs ETF for 20–30% slab taxpayers. Ideal for: Tier 2/3 towns, Jan Dhan users, first-time investors without demat accounts.
📿 Physical Gold — Real Cost
3% GST is added at purchase — so ₹1L investment is really ₹1.03L cost. Making charges 8–25% on jewellery (2–5% on coins). Locker: ₹1,500–₹5,000/yr. LTCG after 2 years at 20% with indexation (Finance Act 2023 removed indexation for many assets but physical gold retained it). Always buy BIS Hallmarked (916/750) gold only.
🏛️ Gold Monetisation Scheme (GMS)
Indian households hold ~25,000 tonnes of idle gold in lockers earning 0%. GMS lets you deposit idle jewellery at designated banks and earn 2–2.5% annual interest, tax-free. Minimum: 10 grams. Gold returned in equivalent gold or cash at maturity. Before Dhanteras/Akshaya Tritiya, check if your idle jewellery could be earning instead of sitting in a locker.
📋
Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGB) — Footnote Only
The government discontinued new SGB issuances after February 2024 with no announced plans to resume. The 2026 Union Budget also clarified that the capital gains exemption applies only to original subscribers holding to maturity — not to secondary market buyers on NSE/BSE. If you hold existing SGBs, continue holding to maturity (8 years) for the tax benefit. Do not buy SGBs on the secondary market expecting tax-free gains.

⚠️ Important Disclaimer — Please Read

Aishwaryamasthu is an educational financial calculator — not SEBI-registered investment advice. This tool is intended solely for financial literacy and illustrative planning purposes. It does not constitute investment advisory services, portfolio management, or a recommendation to buy, sell, or hold any security or financial product. The projections, scores, fund rankings, and metrics shown are estimates based on historical data and assumptions — actual returns may vary materially. Mutual fund investments are subject to market risks. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Data sourced from AMFI (mfapi.in) may have delays or inaccuracies. Before making any investment decision, please consult a SEBI-Registered Investment Adviser (RIA) or a qualified financial planner.

💡 Real Indian Example

⛔
High-Risk Asset Warning
Read before adding Crypto to your Financial Plan
💸
30% Flat Tax on Every Gain
No slab rate, no LTCG benefit. A 15% gross return becomes ~10.5% after tax — similar to large-cap equity but with far higher risk.
🏦
1% TDS on Every Transaction
Deducted on transactions above ₹10,000. Continuously reduces usable capital, even on losing trades.
🚫
No Loss Set-Off Allowed
Crypto losses cannot offset gains from any source — not even other crypto. Each coin is taxed independently.
📉
Extreme Volatility + Regulatory Risk
50–80% drawdowns are common. Bitcoin fell 77% in 2022. India's crypto regulation is unsettled — holdings could become illiquid.

By adding crypto, you confirm you have read and understood the risks above. Projections are illustrative only.
